description: Add a UK visa application schema extraction and dynamic form-generation workflow to VIZA, modeled on the existing DS-160 approach. The run should identify the official UK visa application journey, capture required fields plus all enumerated options and conditional branches, normalize that into VIZA's visa_form_fields / schema flow, and expose a renderable UK visa form path with truthful documentation of any unsupported source-flow gaps.

scopeContract.truthStatement: This run is about official-source-first UK visa form parity: complete field coverage, accurate options, and preserved branching logic are more important than broad but speculative support.
scopeContract.rejectedAssumption: A hand-written UK form or a partially copied field list is NOT acceptable proof of parity. The implementation must be grounded in the official UK application flow and document any gaps explicitly.
scopeContract.designPrinciple: source-truth-over-manual-approximation: preserve the official field structure, requiredness, options, and conditional logic in a machine-readable VIZA schema before optimizing downstream automation.
scopeContract.integrationNotes: Reuse the existing DS-160/dynamic-form patterns where appropriate. Do not hardcode brittle one-off UI-only fields when the underlying schema path can be extended. Do not claim unsupported UK visa categories beyond the canonical v1 journey selected during implementation.

userStories.title: Per-applicant credential vault using Supabase Vault
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need per-applicant secrets (portal logins, 2captcha keys, derived tokens) stored encrypted at rest and decrypted only inside the runner sandbox, so a DB leak does not expose every applicant's credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase Vault (or pgcrypto + KMS) wired in agent-backend with `applicant_secret(applicant_id, key, ciphertext)` table
Read access guarded by RLS such that only service-role + the owning applicant_id can decrypt
Encryption key sourced from env / KMS, never committed
Migration adds `applicant_secret` table; seed script removed for any plaintext secrets
userStories.notes: Replaces ad-hoc .env stuffing. Submission-service must read through this API only.

userStories.title: Submission-service consumes secrets only via the vault
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the Playwright runner to fetch credentials from the vault at job start and never receive plaintext from anywhere else, so credential exposure is bounded to a single trusted code path.
acceptanceCriteria: All `process.env.PORTAL_*` reads in submission-service replaced with `vault.get(applicant_id, key)`
Runner crashes loudly if vault returns null instead of falling back to env
Existing manual-qa harness updated to seed test secrets via vault helper script
docs/manual-qa-prompt.md updated to reflect vault flow
userStories.notes: Run after SECRETS-001.

userStories.title: Secret rotation + audit log
userStories.description: As an operator, I need every secret read/write recorded with applicant_id, requesting service, and timestamp, so we can prove which job touched which credential after an incident.
acceptanceCriteria: `secret_access_log` table records (applicant_id, key, action, actor, ts)
Vault read/write helpers append to log atomically
Admin portal exposes a per-applicant secret-access view (read-only, no values)

userStories.title: Strip plaintext secrets from existing repo + history audit
userStories.description: As a security reviewer, I need to confirm no portal credentials, captcha keys, or service-role keys live in the repo, scripts, or sample .env files, so we are clean before onboarding real clients.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `gitleaks` (or equivalent) over the repo and resolve every finding
Any historical leak documented in a `SECURITY.md` postmortem
Pre-commit hook installed to block future secret commits
userStories.notes: Hard gate before public client launch.

userStories.title: Service-role key boundary review
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need every `createAdminClient()` / service-role usage justified and minimised, so the blast radius of a leaked service-role key is contained.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit of every `createAdminClient()` call site documented in `docs/service-role-audit.md`
Frontend never imports admin client (verified by lint rule)
Server actions that need elevation funnel through a single `withAdmin()` helper

userStories.title: Cloudflare Email Routing baseline on haggstorm.com
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need haggstorm.com configured to receive mail via Cloudflare Email Routing with verified DNS, so we can address per-applicant aliases under a domain we control.
acceptanceCriteria: Email Routing enabled in Cloudflare dashboard for haggstorm.com
MX, SPF, and DKIM records present and validated by Cloudflare
A canary alias `inbox-canary@haggstorm.com` forwards successfully to a maintainer mailbox
DNS state and rollback steps documented in `docs/inbox-cloudflare-setup.md`
userStories.notes: Dashboard ops (enable routing, add canary, verify DNS) must be performed by maintainer; runbook at docs/inbox-cloudflare-setup.md captures every step + rollback. Snapshot table in that doc is the durable record once provisioning runs.

userStories.title: Email Worker that ingests inbound mail to Supabase
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a Cloudflare Email Worker that receives every message to *@haggstorm.com, parses it, and writes a row to Supabase `inbound_email`, so submission-service can read OTPs without IMAP.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker repo path under `viza-be/email-worker/` with deploy script
Worker handler signature `email(message, env)` parses from / to / subject / text / html / message-id
Insert to Supabase `inbound_email(id, to_addr, from_addr, subject, text, html, headers, raw_size, received_at, processed)` via service-role REST
Large bodies (>1MB) stored in R2 and the row references the R2 key
Drizzle migration adds `inbound_email` table with indexes on `to_addr` and `received_at`
userStories.notes: Cloudflare Email Workers free tier (100k/day) covers MVP.

userStories.title: Per-applicant alias generator + reverse map
userStories.description: As a runner, I need each applicant assigned a stable alias `appl-{token}@haggstorm.com` and the inbound table queryable by alias, so I can poll only that applicant's mail.
acceptanceCriteria: Server action `assignApplicantInboxAlias(applicantId)` returns `appl-{ulid}@haggstorm.com` and persists to `applicant.inbox_alias`
`inbound_email.to_addr` is normalised lowercase and joins to applicant
Helper `inbox.waitForMessage(applicantId, predicate, timeoutMs)` polls Supabase realtime
userStories.notes: Helper replaces IMAP polling for new flows.

userStories.title: OTP / confirmation extractor with provider profiles
userStories.description: As a runner, I need a configurable extractor that pulls 6-digit codes, magic links, and confirmation references from inbound mail by sender domain, so flows do not hand-roll regex per country.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests cover at least 5 real (anonymised) sample messages
Falls back to a generic 6-digit-code extractor when no profile matches

userStories.title: Wire Cloudflare inbox into Vietnam + UK runners
userStories.description: As an operator, I need at least two existing runners to use the Cloudflare-backed inbox helper end-to-end, so we have proof the path works before retrofitting all flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Vietnam e-Visa runner reads OTP via `inbox.waitForMessage`
UK runner (when it requires email confirmation) reads via the same helper
Smoke test demonstrates an OTP message landing in `inbound_email` and being consumed under 30s
IMAP fallback documented as deprecated for these two flows
userStories.notes: Pilot before global cutover.

userStories.title: Inbox viewer in client + staff portal
userStories.description: As a client, I need to see emails received on my behalf at my applicant alias, so I can audit what the agency saw and forward anything missed.
acceptanceCriteria: `/client/inbox` lists messages for the signed-in applicant (via RLS)
`/staff/applicants/[id]/inbox` shows the same view for staff
HTML rendering is sanitised (no remote tracking pixels by default)
userStories.notes: Staff route ships at /admin/users/[id]/inbox — repo has /admin/* (no /staff/* yet). Same RLS-driven data path as /client/inbox; will rehome under /staff if/when staff portal lands.

userStories.title: Inbox retention, abuse, and bounce policy
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need inbound messages retained on a finite schedule and bounced when an alias is retired, so we are not a perpetual mail sink.
acceptanceCriteria: Aliases for applicants in terminal status (delivered/cancelled) optionally retired with a tombstone
Worker rejects (with 5xx) messages over 25MB and logs the rejection
Spam score threshold applied (Cloudflare provides spamScore) with quarantine bucket

userStories.title: Terms of Service + Privacy Policy + DPA drafts
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need legally-reviewable ToS, Privacy Policy, and Data Processing Agreement drafts that match what VIZA actually does (handle PII for visa applications across 30 jurisdictions), so we can engage counsel and put them behind signup.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/legal/terms-of-service.md` covers scope of service, fees, refund stance, liability cap, jurisdiction
`docs/legal/privacy-policy.md` enumerates PII categories collected, retention, third-party processors (Supabase, Cloudflare, 2captcha, Stripe, government portals)
Each doc flagged `STATUS: draft — pending counsel review`
All third-party processors listed in a single `subprocessors.md`
userStories.notes: Not legal advice. Required artefact before counsel engagement.

userStories.title: Consent capture + signup gate
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need every applicant to explicitly accept ToS + Privacy + the agency authorisation before any submission, recorded with timestamp, IP, and version hash, so we have evidence of consent.
acceptanceCriteria: Signup form blocks until ToS + Privacy checkboxes are explicitly ticked
Per-application authorisation ("I authorise VIZA to submit on my behalf") captured separately
`consent_event(user_id, doc_kind, doc_version, ip, ua, ts)` table populated
Doc version is the SHA-256 of the rendered markdown to detect drift

userStories.title: PII retention + automated deletion
userStories.description: As a data steward, I need a configurable retention policy with automated deletion of expired applicant PII, so we honour stated retention without manual work.
acceptanceCriteria: Retention windows defined per data class (passport scan, photo, form answers, runner artefacts) in `docs/legal/retention.md`
Cron / scheduled job purges expired rows + R2 objects
Privacy Policy reflects actual code behaviour

userStories.title: Data export + deletion endpoints (GDPR Art. 15 / 17)
userStories.description: As a data subject, I need to download a complete export of my data and request deletion, so we are compliant with GDPR / CPRA / PIPEDA.
acceptanceCriteria: `/client/account/export` produces a ZIP containing JSON of all user data + uploaded files
`/client/account/delete` initiates a verified deletion flow with a 7-day soft-delete window
Privacy Policy enumerates these rights and how to exercise them

userStories.title: PII access audit log
userStories.description: As a data steward, I need every staff/admin read of applicant PII recorded, so we can prove who saw what during an incident.
acceptanceCriteria: Server actions that touch passport / photo / form-answer rows wrap reads in `auditPiiRead(actor, applicant_id, fields)`
Customers can request their own access log via export endpoint

userStories.title: Country-specific compliance addenda
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need a per-country addendum noting any jurisdiction-specific data rules (e.g., India's DPDPA, China's PIPL, EU GDPR, Canada's PIPEDA, Australia's Privacy Act), so counsel review is targeted.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/legal/jurisdictions/{cc}.md` for every country in the schema set with material data rules
Each note flags whether VIZA's current architecture appears compliant or needs work

userStories.title: Pricing model + government-fee pass-through schema
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need a data model that separates agency fees, government fees, and third-party costs (captcha solves, proxy spend) per application, so revenue and unit economics are auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: `order_line(order_id, kind, amount_cents, payee, ...)` table for line items
Drizzle migration applied; seed for the 30 packages with placeholder pricing
userStories.notes: Pricing values placeholder; ops fills real numbers.

userStories.title: Stripe Checkout integration for agency fees
userStories.description: As a client, I need to pay the VIZA agency fee through a familiar Stripe Checkout flow before submission begins, so payment is collected before automation runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Stripe webhook handler processes `checkout.session.completed` and marks the order paid
Application status gates on `order.status = paid` before runner enqueues
Test mode covered by an integration test against Stripe CLI fixtures
Refunds reflected back to order state

userStories.title: Government-fee handling design + per-country routing
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need a documented design for how government fees are paid for each of the 30 jurisdictions (in-portal card during runner, escrow, applicant-direct, etc.), so AU/VN/IT/Schengen can complete their submission lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/payments/government-fee-routing.md` lists each country and the chosen mechanism
Mechanism options enumerated: (a) runner pays with virtual card from escrow, (b) client pays in-portal mid-flow, (c) applicant pays directly via shared link, (d) paper-only no fee
Per-country runner code documents which mechanism it uses
At least one country (suggest Vietnam) has the mechanism implemented end-to-end as proof
userStories.notes: Hardest non-trivial design decision in the system.

userStories.title: Refund + chargeback policy implemented
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a refund flow that respects which costs are recoverable (agency fee yes, government fee usually no), so support staff cannot accidentally over-refund.
acceptanceCriteria: Staff portal action `refund(order_id, line_ids[])` with per-line refundability rules
Refunds executed via Stripe Refund API; webhook reconciles state
Chargeback webhook records dispute and pauses any in-flight automation
Refund policy in ToS reflects code behaviour

userStories.title: Receipts + invoicing
userStories.description: As a client, I need a downloadable PDF receipt and a B2B invoice option, so I can expense the visa cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Receipt PDF generated server-side from order data
Invoice template supports company name + tax ID + VAT line

userStories.title: Tax + currency support per jurisdiction
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need taxation handled correctly per applicant geography (VAT for EU, GST for AU/CA/NZ/SG, sales tax for US), so we are not accidentally evading tax.
acceptanceCriteria: Stripe Tax enabled and verified for at least the EU, UK, AU, CA, US
Tax line surfaced separately in checkout and on invoices
Multi-currency pricing for at least USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, CAD

userStories.title: Operational ledger + revenue dashboard
userStories.description: As a finance owner, I need a daily ledger of payments, refunds, government-fee outflows, and per-package gross margin, so unit economics are visible.
acceptanceCriteria: `/admin/revenue` shows MRR, gross margin per package, refund rate
Stripe payouts reconciled against internal `order` table daily
Discrepancies > $5 alert via the OPS alerting channel

userStories.title: Containerised Playwright runner image
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need the submission-service runner packaged as a deterministic container image with pinned Chromium and stealth plugins, so production runs match dev.
acceptanceCriteria: Image pins Playwright + Chromium + stealth plugin versions
Image runs the existing `_e2e.ts` smoke tests successfully
GitHub Actions builds + pushes to a container registry on main

userStories.title: Job queue for runner work
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need application submissions enqueued and processed by workers so we are not running Playwright inside the API request path.
acceptanceCriteria: BullMQ (Redis) or Cloudflare Queues chosen and documented in `docs/infra/queue.md`
`runner_job(application_id, country, status, attempts, last_error, started_at, finished_at)` table mirrors queue state
Producer: server action enqueues after `order.status = paid`
Consumer: worker process picks jobs and updates state
userStories.notes: Postgres FIFO chosen over BullMQ/Cloudflare Queues for MVP; swap path documented.

userStories.title: Worker pool autoscaling + concurrency limits
userStories.description: As an operator, I need worker concurrency capped per country (so we do not flood a portal) and autoscaled to drain the queue, so SLAs are predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-country max concurrency configured in env / DB
Autoscaling driver targets queue depth (e.g., Fly Machines, Cloud Run jobs, or k8s HPA)
Concurrency violations alert via OPS channel
userStories.notes: Driver target = Fly Machines (production); autoscaler emits per-country desired counts via --json.

userStories.title: Residential / mobile proxy pool integration
userStories.description: As an operator, I need each runner job route through a residential proxy tied to the applicant's claimed location, so portals do not anti-bot us off datacenter IPs.
acceptanceCriteria: Provider chosen (Bright Data / Oxylabs / IPRoyal) and documented
`getProxyForApplicant(applicantId, country)` helper returns a sticky session
Each runner registers proxy use to the artefact bundle
Per-country override: some flows must run from in-country IPs (e.g., CN-via-VFS)
userStories.notes: Bright Data residential pool chosen; sticky-session helper writes to runner_job.metadata.proxy.

userStories.title: Browser fingerprint + session persistence per applicant
userStories.description: As an operator, I need each applicant's runner to use a stable browser fingerprint across runs (canvas, fonts, timezone), so portals do not flag us across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Stealth plugin reads from this table per run
Profile created on first run and reused thereafter

userStories.title: Artefact storage in R2 / Supabase Storage
userStories.description: As an operator, I need each runner job to persist screenshots, HAR files, and submission PDFs to durable storage with signed-URL retrieval, so support can debug a failed run.
acceptanceCriteria: Bucket created with lifecycle rules (retain 90d, glacier 365d)
Helper `artifact.put(jobId, name, body)` returns signed URL
Existing `artifact-storage.ts` refactored to use the new bucket
Staff portal links to artefacts from job detail view
userStories.notes: Lifecycle rules documented for bucket-side config; new path family `jobs/<jobId>/` coexists with legacy.

userStories.title: Production deployment topology documented
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need a single document explaining where every component runs in prod (Vercel for FE, Fly/Cloud Run for runners, Supabase, Cloudflare, Redis, R2), so onboarding and incident response are straightforward.

userStories.title: Application status dashboard for staff
userStories.description: As a staff member, I need a single view of every application with its status, blocking step, and last runner artefact, so I can answer "where is my visa?" without paging an engineer.
acceptanceCriteria: `/staff/applications` lists with filter by status, country, applicant
Detail view shows runner timeline + artefacts + payment state + inbox
userStories.notes: Shipped under /admin/applications (repo has /admin not /staff); same data path.

userStories.title: Runner job log viewer with screenshots
userStories.description: As an engineer, I need to step through every page transition of a failed runner job with screenshot + console log + network request, so I can root-cause anti-bot triggers and selector drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Each step links to screenshot + HAR slice + console output

userStories.title: Failure alerting (Slack / email / push)
userStories.description: As an on-call engineer, I need a job failure to page me with enough context to triage in 60 seconds, so SLAs are real.
acceptanceCriteria: Slack webhook integration with severity levels
Per-failure-class throttling (do not fire 100 alerts when a portal goes down)
Each alert links to the job artefact viewer
userStories.notes: Doc landed at docs/ops/alerting.md (project tree has docs/ops/, no docs/runbooks/ today).

userStories.title: Per-portal canary health check
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a synthetic canary running against each supported portal hourly that detects layout / selector / login changes before real applicants do, so we get advance warning.
acceptanceCriteria: `canary` cron runs each runner's `recon`/`probe` against the live portal
Failures open an OPS alert + a tracking issue

userStories.title: Metrics + KPI tracking
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need success rate, time-to-submit, captcha cost, and proxy cost tracked per country with weekly trend, so we know where to invest.
acceptanceCriteria: Metrics emitted to Supabase or PostHog from the runner
`/admin/metrics` shows the four KPIs by country, weekly bucket
Threshold on success rate < 90% triggers an alert

userStories.title: Runbooks for common failure classes
userStories.description: As an on-call engineer, I need a written runbook per common failure class (CAPTCHA budget exhausted, portal anti-bot trigger, OTP not received, payment dispute, document rejected), so I can resolve incidents without tribal knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: Each runbook follows: symptoms / diagnosis / mitigation / escalation
userStories.notes: 9 runbooks shipped + index; reviewed 2026-05-07.

userStories.title: On-call rotation + escalation policy
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need a published on-call schedule + escalation path so production incidents get a human within 15 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: Schedule published in PagerDuty / Opsgenie / similar
Test page / silence drill executed monthly
userStories.notes: PagerDuty schedule provisioning + first drill TODO post-launch; runbook + escalation tree shipped 2026-05-07.

userStories.title: Photo capture + ICAO compliance check
userStories.description: As an applicant, I need to upload a passport-style photo and have it auto-validated for size, head position, background, and lighting per the destination's spec, so the runner does not get rejected at the portal photo step.
acceptanceCriteria: Client-side capture component (web + mobile-web) with crop guides
Server-side validation against per-country spec (DS-160 / Schengen / IN / etc.)
Failed validation returns a human-readable reason
Spec table in `docs/photo-specs.md` for all 30 jurisdictions
userStories.notes: ML-based ICAO checks (background, head height, expression) deferred — current validator handles deterministic rules (format, size, dimensions).

userStories.title: Passport scan OCR + auto-fill into form
userStories.description: As an applicant, I need to upload a passport scan and have name / DOB / passport number / nationality / expiry auto-extracted, so I do not retype data prone to typos.
acceptanceCriteria: OCR provider chosen (Textract / Google Doc AI / Azure / open-source)
MRZ parsed deterministically; visual fields fall back to OCR
Extracted fields populate the dynamic form for the active package
userStories.notes: Provider = Anthropic Claude vision (already in stack via agent-backend); MRZ TD3 parser + check digits live deterministically alongside OCR fallback.

userStories.title: Supporting document upload + checklist per package
userStories.description: As an applicant, I need a per-package checklist of supporting documents (bank statement, itinerary, employment letter, invitation, photo) with upload slots, so I know what is required before I pay.
acceptanceCriteria: Each slot accepts PDF/JPEG/PNG with size + virus scan
Status indicator per slot (missing / uploaded / accepted / rejected)
userStories.notes: Per-package slot rows seeded by ops; validator covers PDF/JPEG/PNG magic + size; AV scan deferred to a Storage-side ClamAV sidecar.

userStories.title: Per-jurisdiction PDF assembly for paper-channel flows
userStories.description: As a runner, I need to assemble a fillable / printed PDF for jurisdictions where the channel is paper (e.g., Japan tourist, Korea C-3-9, India OCI when relevant), so the agency can mail or hand-deliver to the consulate.
acceptanceCriteria: PDF template registered per package that needs paper output
`paper-renderer.ts` fills the template from the canonical answer set
Output stored in artefact storage and downloadable from staff portal
userStories.notes: Handles JP / KR / others where automation halts at "print and submit". Renderer ships printable declaration-style PDF; AcroForm field-fill (e.g. MOFA Form A) is a sibling renderer follow-on with the same contract.

userStories.title: E-signature capture for agency authorisation forms
userStories.description: As an applicant, I need to e-sign the agency authorisation + any consulate-required power of attorney inline, so the staff does not chase paper signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: Signature canvas component with audit trail (IP, ts, doc hash)
Signed PDF stored in artefact storage
userStories.notes: Audit row binds doc-hash + IP/UA at sign-time; signature PNG stored in submission-artifacts; existing /client/signing flow remains the AU entrypoint.

userStories.title: Document retention + redaction on delivery
userStories.description: As a data steward, I need uploaded documents purged or redacted on a schedule after the visa is delivered, so we are not storing passports forever.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron purges or redacts per schedule
Audit log records every purge event
userStories.notes: Defaults: passport/photo 30d post-delivery; supporting 90d; artefacts 180d. Audited via retention_purge_log.

userStories.title: In-portal applicant chat (extends existing Socket.IO)
userStories.description: As an applicant, I need to chat with VIZA staff (and optionally an AI agent) inside the portal without leaving the application context, so I can ask "why is my application stuck?" without email tag.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-conversation context includes the active application and last runner step
userStories.notes: Staff chat threads ride a separate table (staff_chat_thread/_message) with Supabase realtime; existing /visa Socket.IO namespace stays focused on Companion stream. /admin/chat queue ships; client widget integration is a one-line add to existing chat-client when the human-handoff CTA is wired.

userStories.title: Status update notifications (email + push)
userStories.description: As an applicant, I need to be emailed (and optionally push-notified) on every meaningful application transition, so I do not have to babysit the portal.
acceptanceCriteria: Transition events: paid, runner-started, runner-stopped-for-input, submitted, decision-issued, document-ready
Emails templated and delivered via Resend / Postmark
Mobile push via Expo / OneSignal where the mobile app is in scope
User preferences page lets applicant opt out of non-essential
userStories.notes: Six events templated. Email via Resend; push via Expo (token+provider on prefs row); essential vs opt-out enforced server-side.

userStories.title: Operator takeover for stuck runners
userStories.description: As a staff member, I need to take over a Playwright session that hit an unhandled state (e.g., new ID-verify page, blocked by anti-bot) and finish it manually, so a single new portal change does not stall every applicant.
acceptanceCriteria: Runner emits `needs-human` state with a remote-debug URL
Staff portal exposes the URL behind 2FA
Operator actions logged to the runner artefact bundle
Operator completion writes results back to the canonical answer set
userStories.notes: High-leverage capability for new-portal incidents. 2FA gate enforced via Supabase TOTP factor; remote-debug URL revoked on takeover close.

userStories.notes: 6 MDX articles authored (vn/us/uk/eu/au/in) under docs/help/. Surfaced at /client/help/articles with active-package filter + substring search; chat-suggestion server action returns top-3 hits.
